---
title: "Discussion 11: Analyze a commercial Recommender"
author: "Madina Kudanova"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Introduction

\
The New York Times came up with a sophisticated yet simple solution to help readers navigate the hundreeds of articles published daily. This solution is a recommender system that combines user behavior and article content to deliver personalized article recommendations. This report analyzes the system using scenario design, reverse engineering, and proposes improvements.

##  Design Analysis 

### User's Needs

\
The primary users are readers of The New York Times.\
\
Their goals are:\
1)Quickly find relevant and interesting articles\
2)Stay informed without being overwhelmed\
3)Discover new content aligned with their interests\
\
The recommender system helps by analyzing reading history and suggesting articles that match user preferences, reducing search effort and improving content discovery.

###  NYT's Needs

Its goals are:\
1) Increase user engagement\
2)Retain subscribers\
3)Encourage more time spent on the platform\
\
The recommender system supports these goals by delivering personalized content, increasing the likelihood that users continue reading and interacting with the platform.\
\

### Reverse Engineering the Recommendation System

Based on available information, The New York Times uses a hybrid recommendation system that combines content-based filtering and collaborative filtering.\
\
Content-based filtering uses article features such as keywords, topics, and metadata to recommend similar articles. This allows the system to handle new content effectively.\
\
Collaborative filtering uses reading behavior from similar users to recommend articles that others with similar interests have read.\
\
The current system combines both approaches using a method inspired by collaborative topic modeling. Articles are represented as mixtures of topics, and users are modeled based on their reading preferences. Recommendations are generated by matching users with articles that align with their interests. :contentReference\[oaicite:0\]{index=0}

##   Strengths

1\) Combines multiple approaches for better accuracy\
2)Handles new articles effectively\
3) Adapts to user preferences over time\
4)Scales to large datasets\

## Weaknesses

1)May create filter bubbles\
2)Sensitive to noisy data such as accidental clicks\
3)Limited transparency for users\
4)Can overemphasize certain topics

##  Room for Improvement

1)Provide explanations such as “Recommended because you read X”\
2)Increase diversity of recommendations\
3)Allow users to adjust or reset preferences\
4)Improve handling of weak or accidental signals\
5)Increase transparency in recommendation logic\

## Conclusion

The New York Times recommender system uses a hybrid approach that combines content-based and collaborative filtering to deliver personalized article recommendations. While effective, improvements in transparency, diversity, and user control could further enhance the system and user experience.\
\

## References

Spangher, A. (2015). Building the Next New York Times Recommendation Engine. The New York Times.
